name: ui-skills
description: Opinionated, evolving constraints to guide agents when building interfaces
capabilities: Provides reusable UI component patterns for common interface elements
Enforces consistent design language across generated code
Includes accessibility guidelines and WCAG compliance checks
Offers evolving constraints that adapt to project requirements
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation-only content (SKILL.md) with no executable code. Static analysis flagged URLs and patterns that are false positives - the URLs are GitHub repository references in documentation, not network requests. No security risks detected.
valueStatement: AI agents often produce inconsistent UI code across different sessions. This skill provides opinionated design constraints and patterns to ensure consistent, accessible interface generation every time.
userTitle: Build Consistent User Interfaces with Design Constraints
limitations: Does not include visual design assets or icon libraries
Requires manual integration with existing design systems
Does not generate production-ready CSS or styling code
Focuses on React-based component patterns only

useCases.title: Frontend Developer Building a Design System
useCases.description: Use this skill to generate consistent component patterns that follow established design principles and accessibility standards.

useCases.title: AI Agent Creating UI Components
useCases.description: Enable AI coding assistants to produce UI code that adheres to your team's design constraints and patterns.

useCases.title: Startup Team Standardizing UI Development
useCases.description: Establish a shared vocabulary of UI patterns for small teams without dedicated design resources.

promptTemplates.title: Generate a Basic Button Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the ui-skills patterns, generate a button component with primary, secondary, and disabled states. Include accessibility attributes and focus states.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Form Input with Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply ui-skills constraints to create a text input component with label, placeholder, error state, and validation messaging. Follow WCAG guidelines.

promptTemplates.title: Build a Responsive Navigation Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Using ui-skills patterns, create a navigation component that works across desktop and mobile. Include keyboard navigation and screen reader support.

promptTemplates.title: Design a Data Table with Sorting
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply ui-skills constraints to generate a data table component with sortable columns, row selection, and pagination. Ensure accessibility compliance.

bestPractices: Always define design tokens (spacing, colors, typography) before generating components
Test components with keyboard navigation and screen readers during development
Document component props, states, and usage examples for team consistency
antiPatterns: Hardcoding color values instead of using design system tokens
Ignoring focus states and keyboard navigation in interactive elements
Creating overly complex components that should be split into smaller units

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on React-based component patterns but the design principles apply to any frontend framework.

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual CSS code?
faq.answer: No, it provides component structure and patterns. Styling should be applied using your existing design system or CSS framework.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my existing design system?
faq.answer: Map your design tokens (spacing, colors, typography) to the patterns in this skill before generating components.

faq.question: Can I customize the accessibility requirements?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses WCAG as a baseline but you can specify additional requirements in your prompts.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for mobile-first development?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns support responsive design and mobile-first approaches with appropriate constraints.

faq.question: How often are the design patterns updated?
faq.answer: The patterns evolve based on community feedback and emerging best practices in UI development.
